Visual direction study · 23 routes × 3 screens

Twenty‑three ways DigiMetrics could look & feel.

Each route below reinterprets a reference mood for the product you actually ship — SEO audits, rank tracking, backlinks, social reports and billing. Same data, twenty-three identities, from dark command centers to spatial glass and a full component kit. Browse the thumbnail wall, use the ‹› arrows, and click any screen to zoom in.

23
Directions
69
Screens mocked
3
Per direction
1
Product
Summary — every route as a thumbnail · click to open

How to run this review: score each route on distinctiveness (would you recognise it with the logo removed?), fit (does it suit the surface it’s pitched for?), buildability (could Kit — Direction 13 — express it?), and client impression. Star ★ the routes that survive; your shortlist is saved in this browser.

Direction 01

Summit

A dark command center for people who live in the data all day. Teal signals healthy growth, amber flags what needs attention, and a topographic hero grounds the brand — the climb.

In-app · daily driverSEO teamsDark UI

Direction 02

Aura

Warm, calm and premium — glass panels floating over sand-toned gradients with a single molten-orange orb as the brand's signature. A serif display softens the numbers; this is the face for marketing pages and client-facing reports.

Client-facing reportsMarketing pagesWarm & premium

Type
Georgia display · sans body

Direction 03

Horizon

Frosted glass over a dawn-lit landscape — open, optimistic, calm. Not just the doors: the same glass carries the daily dashboard and the monthly report, so the whole product can live in this light. A green-to-lime gradient drives every primary action.

Auth & onboardingCalm dashboard skin

Direction 04

Noir

Cinematic and image-forward — warm darkness, raking light, a serif wordmark. This is the private client workspace (Tura is the public proof; Atrium the quarterly ceremony): day-to-day retainer work that feels like a case study in progress.

Agency presentationsPremium clients

Direction 05

Vapor

Soft, rounded and approachable — pastel clay tiles, gentle inner-light shadows and plenty of air. This route lowers the intimidation of an analytics suite; ideal for onboarding, the marketing site, and a friendly tool library.

Onboarding & settingsApproachable

Direction 06

Prism

A spatial, VisionOS-style way to move through client work — reports become frosted panes floating in a warm room, browsed on a curved carousel. Where Aura is the printed warmth of the report itself, Prism is the live presentation mode you walk a client through.

Presentation modeClient reviewsWow factor

Direction 07

Terraform

An organic, calm take on the product itself — sage and off-white, soft cards, real landscape photography as cover art. The in-app UI that makes a dense analytics suite feel unhurried and human: dashboard, data sources, reports.

In-app · calm analyticsLight UI

Direction 08

Nebula

Where Lilac is the safe control, Nebula is the same family pushed to showpiece: liquid-chrome spheres, deeper iris on warm sand, glass KPI cards and a built-in assistant. The "AI-powered platform" statement piece — still light, never timid.

AI featuresDashboard + assistant

Type

Direction 09

Vanta

Greyscale, monospaced and confident — a brutalist-tech identity where a single red dot is the only colour. Big type, hard grids, a single red signal dot. For a brand that wants to feel like serious engineering, not another pastel SaaS.

Data-dense opsEngineering feel

Direction 10

Fresh

Bright, generous white space and one bold green — the clean-commerce playbook applied to your product. Report templates and tools are browsed like products on a carousel, with a highlighted "hero" pick. Friendly, high-conversion, marketing-led.

Template store & billingHigh conversion

Harvest

Direction 12

Market

The marketplace layout — bold hero, logo strip, chip filters, a featured rail and a ranked leaderboard. Its own slot vs Fresh: not a store but an integrations hub + performance leaderboards — where the workspace plugs in and where the wins get ranked. Black-and-white with green for gains.

Direction 13

Kit

Not a mood — the build kit, shown working. The same warm editorial system, assembled into real product surfaces: workspace settings, a report-builder flow and billing. Proof that whichever route wins, its components can run the everyday screens.

Direction 16

Verse

The AI-search universe as a dark HUD — cyan glow, engine chips, citation telemetry. Built for the newest surface you sell: where brands appear inside AI answers, tracked like deep-space signals.

Cipher

Direction 19

Lilac

The control route — deliberately the closest to today's DigiMetrics platform: white floating cards, soft shadows, violet accent, one step calmer on lavender. Every other direction should beat this baseline to earn a rebuild; if none does, this is the safe evolution.

Tour stop in the productThe real rank table, guided
DigiMetrics — Design Directions. Twenty-three moods, sixty-nine screens, one product. Mockups are static and illustrative; photography is Unsplash-licensed.
Recommended next step: shortlist 2–3 routes with the ★ buttons, assign each a surface (app · marketing · reports · auth), then build the winner into the component kit (Direction 13).
